rag_rules: Place input envelope assumptions in component type annexes using visible feature names and concrete bounds from the requirement.
Do not generate implementation-level guarantees or assign statements unless explicitly tied to outputs or auxiliary variables in implementation scope.
generic_rules: Assumptions and guarantees must reference only visible, typed symbols from the AADL model—no implicit properties or unexposed ports.
Use boolean expressions for range checks only when the underlying data is numeric and explicitly represented in scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks must be wrapped in `annex agree {** ... **};` and placed in component type scope for assumptions.
Comparison expressions must evaluate to bool; use helper equations (eq) to decompose range conditions into typed boolean variables.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE variables—they are not in scope without explicit port or constant mapping.
Avoid converting Boolean or enumeration ports into numeric range comparisons unless the AADL model defines them as numeric scalars.
